name: Majestic Twelve Group
description: Majestic 12 (MJ-12) is the purported code name of a secret committee of scientists, military leaders, and government officials, allegedly formed in 1947 by U.S. President Harry S. Truman to investigate unidentified flying objects (UFOs) and the Roswell incident. Proponents claim MJ-12 was created to analyze recovered alien spacecraft and their occupants, and to manage the potential implications of extraterrestrial contact. The narrative asserts that MJ-12 operated in utmost secrecy, above the law, and reported directly to the President. The alleged existence of MJ-12 first surfaced in 1984 with the release of a set of documents, later dubbed the 'MJ-12 documents,' to UFO researchers. These documents, circulated as classified government memoranda, detailed the formation and activities of the group. Prominent UFO researchers initially embraced the documents as proof of government cover-up, but subsequent investigations revealed inconsistencies and evidence of forgery. Mainstream historians and scientists largely dismiss MJ-12 as a hoax. The documents have been widely discredited due to inconsistencies in formatting, signatures that do not match official records, anachronistic terminology, and demonstrable forgeries of official government letterhead. The FBI investigated the documents and concluded they were likely fraudulent. Despite the lack of credible evidence and widespread debunking, the MJ-12 narrative continues to circulate within UFO conspiracy circles, fueling belief in government secrecy and extraterrestrial encounters.
subtitle: Alleged secret UFO investigation committee.

claims.claim: MJ-12 was formed by President Truman in 1947.
claims.evidence: The 'MJ-12 documents' including a supposed briefing document for President Eisenhower.
claims.criticism: The documents are widely regarded as forgeries by document experts and historians. Signatures are inconsistent, and the format is not authentic.

claims.claim: MJ-12's purpose was to investigate UFOs and alien technology.
claims.evidence: The documents outline the group's mission to analyze recovered alien spacecraft and manage public perception.
claims.criticism: There is no independent corroboration of the documents' claims. The alleged members of MJ-12 have no record of involvement in such a group.

claims.claim: MJ-12 operates in complete secrecy and above the law.
claims.evidence: The documents describe MJ-12 as having extraordinary powers and reporting directly to the President.
claims.criticism: Such a level of unchecked power within the U.S. government would be highly improbable and would likely have been exposed by whistleblowers or investigative journalists.

keyFigures.name: William Moore
keyFigures.role: UFO researcher who initially publicized the MJ-12 documents.

keyFigures.name: Stanton Friedman
keyFigures.role: UFO researcher who initially supported the authenticity of the MJ-12 documents.

keyFigures.name: Philip J. Klass
keyFigures.role: Debunker and investigator who exposed inconsistencies in the MJ-12 documents.

mythologyConnections: Parallels to stories of secret societies

timeline.event: Alleged formation of MJ-12 following the Roswell incident.
Release of the 'MJ-12 documents' to UFO researchers.
Widespread debunking of the MJ-12 documents and continued belief in the conspiracy.
